title: Kerala Semiya Payasam

subs.name: rice vermicelli for a gluten-free semiya payasam
coconut milk for a vegan version
subs.notes: use 400 ml thick coconut milk + 350 ml water; simmer only 8 minutes

subs.name: condensed milk (half a tin) for extra creaminess
subs.notes: reduce sugar accordingly and add condensed milk off the heat

steps.text: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed saucepan on medium flame. Add cashews and fry for 1–2 minutes until golden. Add raisins and fry for 30 seconds until they plump up. Remove both with a slotted spoon and set aside.

In the same ghee (add a little more if needed), add the vermicelli and roast on medium flame, stirring constantly, for 3–4 minutes until the strands turn golden and smell nutty. Do not let them burn — once golden they darken quickly.
Pour in the milk all at once. Stir immediately to prevent any vermicelli from sticking to the bottom. Bring to a boil on medium-high flame, stirring often.

Reduce the flame to medium-low and simmer for 12–15 minutes, stirring every 2 minutes to prevent the milk from catching at the bottom. The vermicelli will absorb the milk and the payasam will thicken — it should be the consistency of thin custard when hot (it thickens more as it cools).
Add sugar, powdered cardamom and rose water if using. Stir well and simmer for 2 more minutes until the sugar dissolves completely. Taste — the payasam should be sweet enough to stand alone.
Add the fried cashews and raisins and stir through. Serve hot, warm or at room temperature. The payasam thickens considerably as it cools — thin with a splash of warm milk before serving if needed.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: vermicelli → rice vermicelli for a gluten-free semiya payasam (reduce cooking time to 8 minutes); milk → coconut milk for a vegan version (use 400 ml thick coconut milk + 350 ml water; simmer only 8 minutes); sugar → condensed milk (half a tin) for extra creaminess (reduce sugar accordingly and add condensed milk off the heat)
